title: agalinis maritima var. grandiflora
intro: Agalinis maritima var. grandiflora, also known as seaside false foxglove, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the figwort family and is native to the coastal regions of North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome and diarrhea. The health benefits of Agalinis maritima var. grandiflora include its ability to soothe digestive discomfort, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Agalinis maritima var. grandiflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
